mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, compressing corporate margins and triggering a short-term equity sell-off as inflation fears dominate valuation models.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when wage growth (PAYEMS) is decoupled from unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production (INDPRO) rises while housing starts (HOUST) fall signals a supply-led economic expansion that outpaces consumer demand, leading to inventory gluts and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when GDP growth (GDPC1) simultaneously exceeds 3.0% annualized, indicating demand is absorbing supply.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a sustained rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) above its pre-shock baseline drives earnings revisions upward, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as capacity utilization normalizes.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO exceeds 105.0, indicating the market has already priced in the recovery.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer credit balances (RSAFS) reflect increasing household leverage and spending capacity, which directly boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 1-year rolling window, or SPX 5-day returns are uncorrelated with RSAFS momentum during periods of CPIAUCSL > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.50%, the market interprets this as a definitive shift from emergency liquidity provision to normalization, causing a repricing of duration risk and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.50%, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and consumer credit (RSAFS) signal a synchronized contraction in both employment and spending, triggering a sharp negative S&P 500 forward return over the next month as recession risks materialize.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL simultaneously falls below 1.5%, indicating disinflation is offsetting demand weakness.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPIAUCSL falls below 2.0%, real yields compress and equity risk premiums expand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as valuation multiples re-rate upward.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL drops below 2.0% while FEDFUNDS remains above 0.25%, indicating that nominal rate floors override disinflationary valuation support.
